title: Cold rain bivouac delay before nightfall
location: High forest trail below an alpine meadow
route_context: Multi-hour descent with deteriorating rain, falling temperature, and limited daylight.

timeline.label: T-180
timeline.summary: Rain intensified while the team was still above the forest junction.

timeline.label: T-120
timeline.summary: Route progress showed the team was behind the daylight plan but still moving.

timeline.label: T-60
timeline.summary: Replay linked cold rain, slow descent, and remaining daylight into an exposure risk summary.

timeline.label: T-30
timeline.summary: Replay referenced a local option set for controlled bivouac, assisted descent, or timed retreat to the forest junction.

timeline.label: T-0
timeline.summary: Incident package marked delayed exit and exposure concern as daylight ended.

post_incident_evidence.summary: After-action notes said earlier packaging of rain, pace, and daylight would likely have improved shelter and communication choices.
Weather history confirmed falling temperature and persistent rain during the final descent window.

baseline_summary: Baseline tracking recognized the late exit only near nightfall.
replay_summary: Phase 2 replay connected pace, weather, daylight, and a local option set before the exposure incident package.
known_outcome_summary: The party exited later with minor cold exposure concerns; this fixture does not assert that any outcome was certain.

assessment.rationale: Replay may have improved the likely outcome by creating earlier exposure awareness and a bounded decision window before nightfall.
